Excluding the impact of foreign exchange and exceptional items, IndiGo reported a net profit of INR 75,025 million.

Exceptionally sharp rupee depreciation, changes in labour laws and a challenging operatin environment offset the operational profit and the Company reported a net loss of IN million. 2/7

Excluding the impact of foreign exchange and exceptional items, the Company reported a net profit of INR 19,206 million

Scoda Tubes Concoll Highlights

1. Domestic revenue mix declined from ~73% to ~65%, while exports increased from 26% to ~35%, showing stronger international demand and diversification.
2. Company is targeting 25% Revenue growth of conservative basis and margins of 14-15%
3. Piercing plant shutdown due to elevated gas prices impacted operations for roughly 15вАУ17 days which impacted topline by around 20 odd crores.  Starting April the plant commenced operations.
4. Operations affected as they use PNG which is largely imported through Qatar.The prices are still impacting and have risen by 25-30%. 
5. Welded tubes are seeing emerging opportunities in data centres, water treatment, construction and HVAC, which management highlighted as future growth sectors.No orders from Data center yet but management will target a few this year. 
6. Seamless pipe expansion of 10,000 MTPA is now operational and now total capacity is 20,000MTPA.
7. Welded pipe capacity expansion is expected to be commissioned in H2 FY27, with trials likely in Q2 FY27 with a capacity of 8,000MTPA and optimal contribution from FY28 
8. FY26 capex planned at around вВє110 crore and FY27 capex estimated at around вВє41 crore.
9. Of the вВє220 crore IPO proceeds, approximately:
    вВє74 crore remains as bank balance,
     вВє50 crore allocated towards working capital,
    вВє27 crore towards capex.

Peak debt next year could reach around вВє250 crore due to ongoing expansion.

Inventory days have risen to 217 days due to higher gas prices and raw material stocking; management expects this to normalize towards 160 days.

Primary raw material is stainless steel round bars produced from scrap, where prices have increased by 25вАУ30%.

Hollow mill backward integration progressing; management targets 100% captive consumption by FY29 (currently ~70%).

Solar power project of 8.79 MW DC (4.99 MW for seamless, 3.8 MW for welded division) expected to generate ~137 lakh kwh units annually and save roughly вВє8.6 crore in electricity costs.

The company is in the final stages of obtaining Marine approval, which would open opportunities in the Italian marine market, expected within 3вАУ4 weeks.

Welded they palnned to do up to 8 inches, but suppliers wanted upto 16 inches, so they  now they are increasing it to 16 inches 

Capacity for seamless  would be 70% and  welded to be  25% FYвАЩ27 (noted: on 13K capacity)

BHEL tender is delayed till July/Aug from Nov last year.

Deepinder Goyal tells FT that quick commerce rivals are operating in "burn-to-earn" mode, while Eternal's Blinkit has a 50% market share:

"We are profitable at 50 per cent of market share. The rest of the industry is in a burn-to-earn mode, where they are spending $2 bn for $5-6 bn dollars worth of NOV."

Also, Goyal on competition from Amazon in quick commerce:

"If it comes to outspending Amazon, we can......We have seen them compete in food delivery with us. TheyвАЩve shut it down. TheyвАЩre not going to let go of quick commerce so easily. But we still think that we have got a better shot at winning."

COAL INDIA

Coal India's coal gasification push may see вВє60,000 crore investment ; REPORTS 

Coal India's three planned gasification projects could attract investments of up to 60,000 crore as India pushes cleaner coal utilisation

Ashok Leyland: Guidance - Concall Update

Management expressed cautious optimism for FY27, anticipating positive demand drivers for commercial vehicles but acknowledging macroeconomic headwinds like global economic uncertainties, commodity price volatility, and diesel price increases.

FY27 capital expenditure is projected to be between INR750 crores and INR1,000 crores.

Investments in subsidiaries will be need-based, with Hinduja Leyland Finance, Hinduja Housing Finance, and OHM Mobility potentially requiring funds for growth plans, while Switch Mobility India is now profitable and in a comfortable position.

The Defense business is expected to continue its 20% growth trend for the next two to three years, supported by an order book exceeding INR1,500 crores, though execution spans multiple years.

Non-vehicle revenue momentum is expected to continue into Q1 FY27.

Q1 FY27 industry-level commercial vehicle performance is anticipated to be better than Q1 last year, driven by resilient baseline demand, fleet replacement, and GST benefits, despite current macroeconomic challenges.

For Q1 FY27, significant commodity cost increases, particularly in steel, pose a challenge to margins, which the company plans to mitigate through a 1-1.5% price increase and cost control measures, though sustaining the price increase for the full quarter is uncertain.

New product launches are expected to positively impact market share in the tipper and tractor trailer segments starting Q2 FY27.

Management anticipates some moderation in LCV and ICV demand from Q4 FY26 levels, but a relative pickup in heavy-duty trucks, which is favorable for the product mix.

reports.text:
GS on BDL
Sell, TP cut to Rs 1260

Execution remains a concern

Co missed expected execution for seventh year in a row

Channel checks suggest that miss in FY27 was due to:
1) domestic supply chain issues; and

2) unavailability of a component from an overseas supplier.
As a result, contrary to other defense peers under coverage, FY26 revenue declined 27% YoY.

Furthermore, inventory surged 75% YoY at INR 46.3bn (>2x FY26 revenue) mainly due to finished goods waiting for delivery.

On a positive front, estimate FY26 order book at INR 257bn, implying >10x book/bill (best among cos under coverage)

Order inflow in FY27 is likely to stay strong at INR 140bn, however, pick up in execution would be a key monitorable.

Even in exports market, retail demand has been steady, though dispatches have been impacted by logistics disruption
Structural growth drivers for CV demand (price cuts+ replacement of ageing fleet) are intact.

That said, do acknowledge commodity cost inflation, and some moderation in demand, resulting in minor downside revision of our FY27/28E EBITDA estimates.

reports.text:
CITI On GMR Airports

Reported MarQ-26 core profit of Rs1.3bn, vs -Rs2.5bn in MarQ-25 and Rs3.2bn in DecQ-25.

This is 18% lower than consensus where FY26 made 125% of StreetвАЩs FY26E estimate Rs3bn NPAT included one-offs such as cRs1bn claim received by Crete Airport and Rs1.2bn reverse of HyderabadвАЩs tax liability.

MarQ-26 revenue and EBITDA were -1% and -9% below Street, respectively.

Passenger traffic at 3 key airports was -1% QoQ/+1% YoY (Intl and Domestic -2%/+2% YoY).

Combined non-aero revenue saw +6% YoY (Delhi +4%, Hyderabad +14%, Goa +28%) as average spending per pax +5% YoY.

DelhiвАЩs profits -47% QoQ to Rs1.2bn (non-aero per pax -6% QoQ), Hyderabad saw profits +108% QoQ to Rs1.8bn (non-aero per pax flat QoQ), while Goa saw losses narrow 15% QoQ to -Rs0.6bn.

Net debt stood at Rs340bn as of Mar26, compared to Rs345bn in Dec25.

**US Market Outlook**
- S&P 500 earnings momentum remains exceptionally resilient
- CY2026 EPS estimates upgraded from USD 296 to USD 339 over the past year
- CY2026 earnings revision trend at +10%, second-best upgrade cycle in 8 years
- Earnings fundamentals continue validating market rally beyond valuation expansion

**Mega-Cap Technology Trends**
- Magnificent 7 continue leading US earnings growth
- Aggregate revenue growth for mega caps at ~30% versus mid-single digits for broader market
- Nvidia remains standout performer with triple-digit revenue and EBIT growth
- Alphabet, Amazon, Meta and Microsoft continue posting superior profitability trends
- CY27 EPS growth for Magnificent 7 expected to moderate toward ~15%, converging with broader S&P 500 growth

**Sectoral Earnings Rotation**
- Industrials, Technology and Energy witnessing strongest earnings upgrades for CY2027
- Positive revisions seen in Dow, Intel, Seagate, Chevron and Devon Energy
- Signals improving cyclical recovery and capex-led expansion
- Energy and IT sectors show highest proportion of earnings upgrades

**Weakness & Downgrades**
- Consumer Discretionary and Communication Services witnessing maximum downgrade pressure
- Rate-sensitive and consumer-facing businesses remain under pressure
- Real estate-linked companies, cruise operators and media businesses seeing estimate cuts

**Market Breadth & Strategy View**
- Market leadership still concentrated in mega-cap technology
- Earnings breadth gradually improving beyond AI-led rally
- Selective cyclicals appear attractive due to improving estimate momentum
- Industrials, Energy and semiconductor-linked businesses remain preferred themes

**Key Data Points**
- S&P 500 CY27 earnings upgrades:
  - Magnificent 7: +4.4%

  - S&P 500 excluding Mag 7: +1.4%
- S&P 500 MarвАЩ26 earnings growth stood at 13.0% YoY

- Revenue growth improved steadily to 7.5% YoY in MarвАЩ26

MORGAN STANLEY ON CYIENT

Maintain Underweight, Target price at вВє900

Cyient Semiconductors raised strategic funding at around $500 mn valuation

Company to use proceeds for R&D, infrastructure and working capital

Management highlights strong semiconductor ecosystem partnerships and qualified pipeline

Brokerage does not assign equity value to semiconductor business currently

Break-even timeline for semiconductor business may be pushed out by a few quarters

Value creation expected to be gradual and execution dependen

MOTILAL OSWAL ON HITACHI ENERGY INDIA

Maintain Neutral with target price of вВє32,000 (earlier вВє27,000)

Revenue and PAT beat estimates, while EBITDA remained in line

Another round of capex announced to cater to strong demand

Brokerage raised FY27/FY28 EPS estimates by ~8%/~6%

Domestic pipeline expected to remain the key growth driver

Export growth supported by the company's three-el pronged strategy

Margins likely to recover on better pricing and improved product mix

Brokerage expects revenue/EBITDA/PAT CAGR of ~32%/~47%/~43% over FY26-28E

ELARA CAPITAL ON LENSKART

Initiate Buy, Target price at вВє615

Brokerage sees Lenskart as a category-defining eyewear retail leader

Expects 25% revenue CAGR and 38% EBITDA CAGR over FY26-29

Store economics remain among best in Indian retail with 10-12 month payback

Same-store sales growth running above 20%

International business contributes 42% of revenue with presence across 10+ countries

Acquisitions of Owndays and Meller expected to improve ASP mix and premium positioning

Brokerage values company at 50x FY28 EV/EBITDA

KRN Heat Exchanger and Refrigeration - Interesting filing

https://x.com/BeatTheStreet10/status/2059502008525291578?s=20

Promoter says they plan to sell 10L shares post QIP completion, which is going to take place in the next two months

Generally, promoters do not disclose, but one of the regulations of SEBI says that if you plan to sell promoter stake post a significant event, you need to take Pre Clearance Approval from SEBI under PIT Regulations

Promoters have been avoiding pre-intimating it under the guise of "earlier, there was no plan"

MOTILAL OSWAL ON CONTAINER CORPORATION

Maintain Buy with target price of вВє560 (earlier вВє600)

Weak Q4 performance, with realizations and margins remaining under pressure

Brokerage cut FY27 and FY28 EBITDA estimates by ~5-7%

Remains watchful of how Dedicated Freight Corridor connectivity 100001 translates into incremental volume growth

Expects revenue/EBITDA CAGR of ~9%/~12% over FY26-FY28

According to an investigation by  The Indian Express, HDFC Bank allegedly routed around вВє45 crore as вАЬmarketing/sponsorship spendвАЭ to Maharashtra State Road Development Corporation in order to effectively provide higher interest on deposits than permitted under RBI norms.

https://indianexpress.com/article/express-exclusive/hdfc-bank-camouflaged-crores-as-marketing-spend-to-pay-higher-interest-to-state-firm-10709603/

MORGAN STANLEY ON AMARA RAJA BATTERIES

Maintain Underweight , Target price at вВє875

OEM volumes grew 30% YoY while replacement volumes rose 5-6% YoY in Q4

Weak mix, alloy costs, sulfuric acid inflation and rupee depreciation impacted margins

Company took 5-6% price hikes in tranches during Q4

Key margin drivers include recycling ramp-up and higher in-house tubular battery mix 

Customer qualification plant for new energy business expected to scale by Q2FY27

Technology transfer from Gotion Hi-tech remains on hold

Brokerage prefers Exide over Amara Raja due to slower progress in new energy business

NUVAMA ON CONTAINER CORPORATION

Upgrade to BUY from Hold with target price of вВє570 vs вВє600

Weak quarterly performance impacted by higher empty container movement

Market share remained stable QoQ

Export-Import segment expected to remain weak in the near term

Western Dedicated Freight Corridor likely to drive a structural tailwind

Company seen as well placed to benefit from the structural shift in freight logistics

Brokerage cut FY27E/FY28E EBITDA estimates by ~6-7%

NUVAMA ON YATHARTH HOSPITAL

Maintain BUY with target price of вВє950 vs вВє920

Q4FY26 revenue and EBITDA were in line with estimates

PAT beat estimates by ~5%

EBITDA margin stood at ~23.4%, ~34 bps below estimate

Company remains well positioned for sustained high growth

Existing hospitals continue to drive growth with robust occupancy levels

Management plans to expand capacity to 5,000 Beds

Brownfield expansion strategy provides comfort on Beu execution and returns

Improving governance standards remain encouraging

Brokerage expects revenue/EBITDA CAGR of ~30%/~33% over FY26-28E

Stock valued at ~20x H1FY28E EV/EBITDA

MAINTAIN NEUTRAL, TARGET PRICE 3343

JEFFERIES ON ENTERO HEALTH

BUY, TARGET PRICE INCREASED TO 1500 FROM 1350

JP MORGAN ON HYUNDAI 

OVERWEIGHT, TARGET PRICE 2135

Domestic demand likely to remain strong in the near term

Multiple drivers of exports in the near and medium term

Ambition to have EV mix at least in line with or higher than the industry

The company has multiple levers to protect margins

**LG ELECTRONICS INDIA вАУ EXPORTS & PREMIUMISATION DRIVING GROWTH**  

**Brokerage View**
- YES Securities reiterated BUY rating
- Target price set at вВє1,776
- Implies 19.2% upside from CMP вВє1,489
- Valuation based on 45x FY28 EPS

**Growth Outlook**
- Management guiding mid-teen revenue growth for FY27
- Exports expected to become key growth driver
- Premiumisation increasing average selling prices
- Revenue/EBITDA/PAT CAGR estimated at 14%/25%/26% over FY26вАУFY28

**Export Expansion**
- Large-capacity refrigerator exports started in Q1FY27
- Presence expanding across 22 countries
- Side-by-side refrigerators gaining traction globally
- Export business delivering superior margins and FX hedge

**Home Appliances Performance**
- Segment revenue grew 5.7% YoY in Q4FY26
- Premium washing machines and refrigerators saw healthy demand
- Dishwashers emerged as a strong growth category
- EBIT margin stood at 11.9%

**Home Entertainment Strength**
- Segment revenue increased 19.6% YoY
- Large-screen television demand remained strong
- OLED TV market share maintained at 60%
- EBIT margin came at 13.4%

**Premium Product Momentum**
- Strong traction in 5-star ACs and French-door refrigerators
- Essential Series gaining market acceptance
- New product launches across premium categories underway
- Cricket World Cup boosted premium TV demand

**Localization & Manufacturing**
- Localization improved to 55.2% in FY26
- Targeting annual localization improvement of 1вАУ2%
- Sri City plant investment of вВє50 Bn progressing
- Phased commissioning expected from late CY26

**Pricing & Margins**
- Company implemented price hikes to protect margins
- Additional price hikes planned from July onwards
- Commodity inflation and INR depreciation impacted profitability
- Margins expected to remain in low double digits

**Q4FY26 Performance**
- Revenue grew 8.1% YoY to вВє80,537 Mn
- EBITDA stood at вВє9,455 Mn
- EBITDA margin at 11.7%
- PAT came at вВє6,928 Mn

**Balance Sheet & Cash Position**
- Cash balance stood at вВє44.76 Bn
- Working capital at вВє22.73 Bn
- Debt-free balance sheet maintained
- FY26 capex stood at вВє5.5 Bn

**Future Triggers**
- Export growth expected to cushion domestic slowdown
- RAC demand improved during April and May
- Higher localization to support margins
- Sri City expansion to aid future capacity growth

Source: YES Securities

Jefferies India Strategy

Fiscal Impact - Non-Defense Capex growth may be flat

Higher petroleum and fertilizer subsidies and lower oil tax revenues is likely to impact government fiscal

Overall impact could be at Rs 1.25-1.5 trillion to be shared with states /oil companies

Believe that this will need to be offset by lower expenditure elsewhere most likely non-defense capex - which may make it flat year-on-year in FY27

Rikhav Securities Limited Reports EBITDA of вВє32.53 Cr & PAT of вВє18.96 Cr in FY26

Rikhav Securities Limited, one of the leading diversified stock market services providers has announced its Audited Financial Results for H2 & FY26.

H2 FY26 Financial Highlights
* Total Income of вВє 1604.95 Cr
* EBITDA of вВє 6.57 Cr
* PAT of вВє 1.20 Cr
* EPS of вВє 0.31

FY26 Financial Highlights
* Total Income of вВє 1991.62 Cr
* EBITDA of вВє 32.53 Cr
* PAT of вВє 18.96 Cr
* EPS of вВє 4.95

Financial & Business Updates FY26
- Invested вВє5.40 Cr in technology infrastructure and software during FY26, leading to higher depreciation
- Profitability impacted by вВє22.93 Cr loss from share trading activities
- Unrealised SLBM profit of вВє4.33 Cr not recognised in FY26 financials and expected to be realised in upcoming quarter
- Current liabilities increased mainly due to SLBM borrowings of вВє47.34 Cr

Currently, the stock is trading at вВє47.40, with a Mcap of 181.50 Cr. (As on 23rd May 2026)

**MINDA CORPORATION вАУ PREMIUM ELECTRONICS & EV GROWTH MOMENTUM**  

**Brokerage View**
- Axis Securities maintained BUY rating
- Target price set at вВє710
- Implies 23% upside from CMP вВє575
- Valuation based on 28x FY28E EPS

**Order Book Strength**
- Lifetime order book crossed вВє10,000 Cr
- Q4FY26 new wins worth ~вВє3,500 Cr
- Around 20% orders linked to exports
- Strong visibility across 48вАУ60 month execution cycle

**Premium & EV Expansion**
- Won lifetime sunroof order worth ~вВє1,050 Cr
- Mass production expected within 4вАУ5 months
- Expanded TFT cluster portfolio from 3-inch to 15-inch displays
- EV focus strengthened through Turntide Technologies JV

**Technology & Innovation**
- Filed 11 new patents during Q4FY26
- Total patent filings reached 330
- 147 patents already granted
- Over 950 engineers across global R&D centres

**Export & Global Growth**
- Export-led strategy gaining momentum
- Facilities in Vietnam & Indonesia supporting expansion
- Pune die-casting plant positioned as export hub
- Diversifying beyond domestic auto cycle

**Flash Electronics Performance**
- Associate Flash Electronics revenue at ~вВє493 Cr
- EBITDA surged 106% YoY to вВє89 Cr
- PAT increased 137% YoY to вВє49 Cr
- Export programs and EV portfolio driving growth

**Q4FY26 Performance**
- Revenue grew 29% YoY to вВє1,704 Cr
- EBITDA increased 33% YoY to вВє203 Cr
- EBITDA margin improved to 11.9%
- PAT jumped 138% YoY to вВє124 Cr

**Margin & Operating Leverage**
- EBITDA margins guided above 11%
- Premiumisation improving profitability
- Automation & AI-driven efficiencies supporting margins
- Commodity inflation managed through pass-through mechanisms

**Capex & Capacity Expansion**
- FY26 capex stood at record вВє413 Cr
- FY27 capex guidance at вВє400вАУ450 Cr
- New electronics and die-casting facilities underway
- Expansion focused on EV and export opportunities

**Future Outlook**
- Management targets 1.5x industry growth
- Revenue CAGR of 16вАУ18% expected over FY26вАУFY28
- EBITDA & PAT CAGR projected at 22% and 30%
- Vision 2030 targets вВє17,500 Cr topline

**Company Snapshot**
- Market capitalization at вВє13,729 Cr
- FY28E Revenue estimated at вВє8,601 Cr
- FY28E PAT estimated at вВє606 Cr
- Debt-to-equity expected to improve to 0.4x

Source: Axis Securities

@Wealthcreator7

Kotak Inst Eqt on Hindalco
Reduce, TP Rs 1100

4QFY26 consolidated EBITDA beat estimates across both copper and aluminum divisions.

NovelisвАЩ reported EBITDA remains impacted by Oswego, which should restart hot mill ops in early June 2026вАФwith normalization likely in 2HFY27E.

Margins should further increase in the aluminum division on elevated LME in 1QFY27E, only partially offset by higher input costs.

Coal mine cost benefit is likely to accrue only from FY2028.

Commodity price tailwind should offset project execution risks and back-ended growth over FY2027-29E.

HSBC on Hindalco
Buy, TP Rs 1430

India aluminum EBITDA +13% q-o-q; earnings across businesses should further improve

FY26 net debt increase partially driven by one-time factors; debt should decline materially from FY27 year-end

Increase EBITDA estimates by 2-6%

CITI on Hindalco
Neutral, TP Rs 1170

At Rs101.8bn (6% ahead), 4Q consol EBITDA rose 6% yoy/28% qoq

QoQ increase is attributed to better performance across divisions.

India upstream: Rs54.5bn vs. Rs48.3bn in 3Q; India downstream: Rs2.6bn vs. Rs2.3bn; Copper: Rs9bn vs. Rs6bn; Novelis: Rs42bn vs. Rs31bn.

India upstream EBITDA/t was $1,756 vs. $1,572 in 3Q and should continue to rally (spot $3,650), offset partly by 5% higher costs

Est debt should peak at $8.2bn in FY27 & fall thereafter ($6.8bn as of Mar26); estimate net D/EBITDA at 1.6x vs. 1.8x in Mar26
Global team is structurally bullish aluminium; CY27 estimate is $3,800

Maintain Neutral, given LME upside from spot may not be significant

Potential upsides such as (1) Bay Minette profitability, (2) higher India smelting capacity, and (3) captive coal are back-ended.

@Stocksip

Jefferies on Dalmia
Buy, TP Rs 2445

Dalmia has signed an agreement to acquire 5.2MTPA/3.3MTPA Cement/ Clinker assets from Jaypee/Adani at an implied EV/T of ~USD70

Deal revives Dec-22 agreement & is likely to increase competition in Central region

While assets are old & require refurbishment, the M&A provides Dalmia entry into a new region.

Mgmt expects faster ramp-up aided by prior exposure; AdaniвАЩs decision to divest, rather than house assets in Ambuja, appears balance-sheet driven.

@Stocksip

CITI on Dalmia
Buy, TP Rs 2450

DBL Acquires JPAвАЩs Central India Assets for ~$60/t

This acquisition helps: DBLвАЩs expansion targets and India diversification.

Further, any progress on their goals вАУ maximizing ROCE from all assets, improving utilization, price and product premiumization focus, continued cost reduction could yield favorable returns. EV/t at $60 is inexpensive

Concerns around MF units have resurfaced, which could keep stock range bound pending clarity.

@Qoutes90

CLSA on Aurobindo
Downgrade to hold, TP raised Rs 1490

Reported an in-line quarter driven by strong revenue growth in Europe; US declined YoY.
Margins (in line) were lower YoY owing to higher opex.

It expects US$2bn revenue from the US in two years (vs. US$1.6bn in FY26), double-digit growth in Europe in FY27, and 21% consolidated margin in FY27

While it expects new launches, portfolio expansion and acquisitions to lead overall growth, expect the full impact of these initiatives only from FY28

BoFA Sec on Aurobindo
Buy, TP Rs 1700

While ARBPвАЩs US sales were 8% lower QoQ (vs BofAe flattish) on soft seasonality, company reported an inline EBITDA of Rs17.5Bn (20% margin).

This was supported by the 160bps QoQ gross margin expansion largely on Pen-G plant ramp-up.

While $2Bn US revenue run-rate (vs $1.6Bn in FY26) depends on Lannett deal closure (2QFY27), 21% margin guidance is 50-100bps higher than street/BofA estimates.

@Stocksip

GS on Aurobindo
Buy, TP Rs 1550

4Q broadly in line with estimates (+2% vs. GSe), with sales/EBITDA growing by +6%/+1% yoy driven by strong growth in Europe (+29% yoy) and growth markets (+25% yoy) partially offset by a slower US which is still seeing impacts of the gRevlimid ramp-down (-13% yoy).

EBITDA margin came in steady at 20.3% (-16bps vs. GSe), as higher GMs (+216bps yoy) were offset by higher employee costs

Co aspires to achieve high single digit topline growth (ex-gRevlimid) while guiding to maintain margins at 21%+ mark in FY27

@Stockupdate9

Nuvama on Aurobindo
Buy, TP Rs 1680

Reported in-line revenue/EBITDA, but a 5% miss on PAT
Gross margin was better than estimate, while EBTDA margin was broadly in-line

Weak US growth was offset by strong growth in Europe and growth markets

Remain optimistic on AurobindoвАЩs growth/margin prospects as the company is nearing an inflection point of operating leverage in Pen-G and TheraNym CMO unit.

Aurobindo is also set to benefit from growth in Europe and Lannett closure

@Stockupdate9

DAM Cap on Aurobindo
Buy, TP Rs 1767

CoвАЩs steady operating performance over multiple qtrs. reinforces view that it is best placed to any leverage generic industry tailwinds.

Aurobindo is stepping on gas with growing investments in biosimilars, peptides, injectables, etc.

Innovative biologics CMO for MSD adds another medium-term growth engine.

Recent Lannett acquisition seems to be another value accretive M&A

Anticipate meaningful impact of recent big-ticket investments (PenG, China, granulation, injectables) to be visible in coming quarters along with capex intensity easing.
